item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Cow wasps."

"Cow wasps?"

loads his rifle and gets back on the tractor "Cow wasps."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yah, the last little bit about milking Jason? Perfectly done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ¡Vácame Dios! ¡No puedo zumbarlo de verdad!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One thing that bugs me is I don't see how this could be euclid instead of safe. It's overall beneficial to the host, with the only downside being sterilization. MrGrillo's post above also brings up a valid criticism. Lastly, this article doesn't stand out from all of the other bee-related ones, at least in my opinion. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
<rounderhouse> .s hole-y
<jarvis> rounderhouse: SCP-3863: Hole-y cow! I can't bee-lieve it! (written 14 days ago by Uncle Nicolini; rating: +55) - http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-3863
<•XilasCrowe> if this isn't about a cow that has a symbiotic relationship with a swarm of bees that uses the cow as their nest I will be severely disappointed
<rounderhouse> XilasCrowe: have you already read the article
<•XilasCrowe> I have not
<rounderhouse> XilasCrowe: that's it exactly
<•XilasCrowe> oh

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You've got Iron capitalized; I think it should be lower case?

I like this. It's short, weird, and a little gross — but in an almost cutesy sort of way. I feel like there's more you could have done with this (bees are fascinating complex social animals who's collective hive constitutes a type of brain all its own), and I'm a little leery about the Three Portlands reference (though I can see why you did it; implying there's an actual market for this), but all in all, it's a fun, agile concept that gets in and out quickly.

item.title: Re: Honey I milked the kids
item.content:encoded: Eusocial insects are inherently pretty weird?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad to see this posted!

As someone who regularly rescues bees from the sidewalk, I felt pretty grossed out by this. A nice short and snappy and still hecking weird as heck work. +1 from me.

item.title: Inconsistent
item.content:encoded: you say it targets mammals but then say "their udders will engorge as if they were feeding offspring," which doesn't sense for a human… it makes it sound like its just cows, goats, etc

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hot damn that bit about the Foundation putting a ring in is excellent and I am now beating myself up over not thinking of it. Ill be adding it briefly.

As for the bit with Wilson's, I get you. I guess it just comes down to my headcanon being one which facilitates for such relations. Ho hum.

In any case, thanks for reading and providing your feedback, it is highly valued as always! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the very first image is actually not at all a shop. Its a fistulated cow, which have holes cut into them in order to access their stomachs and grab bacteria/rumen.

Well holy shit, my world got a bit darker.

2- the "other goi handles it" is a part of other articles

And it hasn't made a lick of sense to me in them either. I have to be really into something to overlook their inclusion.

Collapsible Stuff

Okay. See, this makes sense as to why the image looked so fake. You're trying to sell me on that hole being a borehole or somesuch, but while in looking for something that appears natural, there's a giant plastic ring around the site, which doesn't look like it was organically formed.

If anything, maybe noting the presence of that ring in the caption as being, er, something the SCPF implanted, it wouldn't seem as uncanny.

Unnerving and makes my stomach churn, but I will say I still think this would be better if it had more to it with the Three Portlands aspect involving WWS, since up until now WWS hasn't had any involvement with Three Portlands.

I Still think MC&D would have been a better pick for the GoI here instead of WWS though; However, you did a good job with the article so I'm not complaining.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Though all your criticism is completely valid and i acknowledge it, I will say two things
1- the very first image is actually not at all a shop. Its a fistulated cow, which have holes cut into them in order to access their stomachs and grab bacteria/rumen. The only edited image here is the red tint added to the comb.
2- the "other goi handles it" is a part of other articles and a result of the relationship wws has with the foundation, which pretty much boils down to "we partially fund these people and they contain any safe-euclid animal anomalies within the boring nexus"

Im not excusing anything here, just clarifying some points.

ETA: To expand on the fistulated cow thing, the issue with the image probably lies within the lack of good quality CC compliant images of fistulated cows. Check the collapsible bellow for more images which are woefully not CC compliant and therefore couldn't make it to the article.

+ show block
– hide block


Link to the Wikipedia Article on the matter
Though there is that one image on the article, I didn't feel as if it could be workable for it as it's too close. I suppose a way to improve this whole ordeal would be to remove the image altogether, huh? :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It feels like SCP-2401, but stripped of everything that made that one stand out: 'the first image here looks like a clear 'shop, and at the same time, I can't even tell what I'm supposed to be looking at that was resolved; the whole process is oh-so painless — and actually beneficial to the host, so I'm not unnerved, disgusted, or upset about the process… add in a wacky containment set-up wherein another group is trusted with handling it, and then we pack up their milk and sell it in another universe and I'm taken so far out of the realm of believability that I'm not sure what I was supposed to get out of the read in the first place.

item.title: Re: Nico this is absolutely cursed
item.content:encoded: Oh god oh fuck you might be right.

item.title: Re: Nico this is absolutely cursed
item.content:encoded: Bee careful Hawks that weird feeling could bee the bees.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicolini, friend, you remind me of myself. I'm so proud of you. In all seriousness though I did like the use of GOIs in this article. I want more worldbuilding like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
oh yeah I forgot stuff was weird there it’s fine to sell this product….

I still believe the foundation, a very careful organization, wouldn’t attempt to sell this stuff… it’s mkre likely they would keep it in storage and during the possibility they had too much of this stuff just drop it in the incinerator with SCP-2149 so those babies can have something to cheer them up

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The way I see it, they need to keep milking these things or else they suffer physical harm (as is the case with most dairy animals) and what are they going to do with all this non-anomalous honey? Might as well let WWS sell it within Portlands, where stuff is already weird anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hold up…. HARVEST AND SELL? SELL FRIGGEN BOOB HONEY? where can I get some?

+1

(Actually I don’t think the foundation would let people sell, or sell themselves, possibly anomalous honey)

(Still doesn’t stop me from contacting Roger tarpan to get me some)

item.title: Nico this is absolutely cursed
item.content:encoded:
This is, by far, the most disgusting thing I read on the site. It made my insides feel weird just by reading it. Yet I know the intention was to make something gross, and the fact that it succeeds with this (perhaps it succeeds too well), I just had to upvote for that perfectly nasty execution.

But don't get me wrong, it makes me want to gag and I hate its existence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: holy bee cows batman

item.title: Re: What if cows was BEES?
item.content:encoded: I agree, it was a good article. The images were a great touch as well.

item.title: Honey I milked the kids
item.content:encoded: I don't really get why there are so many bee-themed anomalies, but I'm not complaining.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who is this Jason Corthon fellow and why does he seem so familiar?

item.title: What if cows was BEES?
item.content:encoded: Short, gross, wonderfully done. High quality article all around. +1

item.title: Honey lactation, Bees, and Cows, oh my!
item.content:encoded:
This originally started as a rewrite of a S2 article before it turned into something else completely.

I'd like to thank Zyn, DarkStuff, Elogee FishTruck, and Weryllium for looking over this very gross thing.

image attribution this way

– hide block

comb ( CC 3.0 + edits by me)
cow ( CC 3.0)
bees ( CC 3.0)
